_doc: Pricing hierarchy per Valuation Policy v1.0 Section 6. The 'hierarchy' array defines fallback order — the system tries each source type in sequence, falling through on failure or staleness.

A1.description: Smart contract convertToAssets or equivalent (Section 6.1). No oracle hierarchy — value is deterministic on-chain.

A2.description: Off-chain yield-bearing tokens. Oracle hierarchy per Section 6.2. Stale if >2x expected update frequency.

A3.description: Private credit vault tokens. Manual accrual from contractual terms (Section 6.3). On-chain TP is cross-reference only.

B.description: PT tokens. Linear amortisation per individual lot (Section 6.4). AMM price is cross-reference only.

C.description: LP positions. Decompose into constituents, price each per its category (Section 6.5). PT in LP uses AMM implied rate, not lot amortisation.

D.description: Leveraged positions. Net = Collateral value - Debt value. Each side priced per its token's category (Section 6.6).

E_par.description: USDC-pegged stablecoins (USDC, DAI, PYUSD, USDS, AUSD, RLUSD). Valued at par ($1.00). Depeg check per Section 9.4.

E_oracle.description: Non-USDC-pegged stablecoins (USDT, USDe, USDD, USX). Oracle-priced per Section 6.7.

F.description: Governance tokens, rewards, other (Section 6.8). Kraken is tier 1 per Final Terms.

divergence_tolerances._doc: Maximum divergence between Primary and Verification Sources per Appendix B
